evidence_quotes.quote: What if they want to go into Apple's direction and force developers to pay to distribute their apps.
This would mean that android would not work without Google's approval.

Not only would all developers have switched to registering with Google and distributing their apps on the Google Play Store, there would be no alternative to distribute their apps, since all app installations need to be verified with Google.

paragraphs.text: Recently, Google announced that they will start blocking the ability to install apps from developers that are not registered with them. This will prohibit a lot of developers from developing their own apps and installing them on their own devices. Some people buy android phones due to the less restrictive nature of the operating system. Having to register with Google should not be necessary. Furthermore, this ban on installing apps that are not verified with Google brings other problems with it. This would increase Google's monopoly by incentivizing developers to only release their apps via Google Play Store, since that would probably be the easiest way to be registered with Google. Google could also just block a developer and their apps if Google does not like their content or their history, prohibiting the developer's career. An additional problem could be more regional. What if a Google service is restricted (e.g. China), where local developers distribute through alternative stores? This would mean that android would not work without Google's approval. Which also means that Google has a monopoly on all android phones in that region. Additionally, if all the distribution goes through one company, it would mean that there is a single point of failure. Google already has experienced some data leaks, and some people are just not comfortable with Google and its security. Forcing people to accept that should not be allowed. My last point, Google could start their ban by just allowing registered developers to distribute their apps for free. However, what stops Google from implementing costs later on? What if they want to go into Apple's direction and force developers to pay to distribute their apps. Not only would all developers have switched to registering with Google and distributing their apps on the Google Play Store, there would be no alternative to distribute their apps, since all app installations need to be verified with Google.
